262	def run_study(
263	    plan: StudyPlan,
264	    *,
265	    gate_handler: GateHandler,
266	    tracer: Tracer | None = None,
267	    tracker: ExperimentTracker | None = None,
268	    versioner: DataVersioner | None = None,
269	    clock: Clock | None = None,
270	    secret: bytes | None = None,
271	    trail: ProvenanceTrail | None = None,
272	    verify_trail: bool = True,
273	) -> StudyResult:
274	    """Run one study end-to-end through its governed phase sequence. See module docstring.
275	
276	    ``gate_handler`` is **required** (no default), so governance review is a conscious choice —
277	    pass :func:`auto_approve` to opt out of human review explicitly, or an operator handler that
278	    can block/park. ``tracer`` defaults to :class:`OtelTracer` (real spans if OpenTelemetry + a
279	    provider are present, a graceful no-op otherwise). ``tracker`` (optional) logs params/metrics
280	    to an experiment store, best-effort (a tracker failure never aborts the run). ``versioner``
281	    (optional) content-addresses the source so the trail cites retrievable bytes. ``clock``
282	    injects a deterministic clock for the provenance timestamps only (ordering checks always use
283	    real time). ``secret`` keys the internal provenance trail with HMAC (forgery-resistant);
284	    mutually exclusive with passing your own ``trail``. ``trail`` may be a caller-owned
285	    :class:`ProvenanceTrail` so a run that *raises* still leaves the caller the partial trail.
286	
287	    Raises ``IngestionError`` on a bad source, ``FirewallViolation``/``PreRegistrationError`` on
288	    a methodological breach, ``GateError`` if a gate blocks, and ``ValueError`` on a malformed run.
289	    """
290	    tracer = tracer or OtelTracer()
291	    if trail is not None and secret is not None:
292	        raise ValueError("pass either a caller-owned trail OR a secret to key a new one, not both")
293	    if secret is None and trail is None:
294	        # An engine-created unkeyed trail is tamper-evident (a naive edit breaks the SHA-256
295	        # chain) but NOT forgery-resistant: an adversary with write access can edit an entry and
296	        # recompute the whole genesis-rooted chain. That is fine for local reproducibility but
297	        # insufficient for an audit trail that crosses a trust boundary. Flag the insecure
298	        # default rather than letting it pass silently (pass 3, #F-049); pass a `secret` to key
299	        # the trail with HMAC, or `clock=`-driven deterministic repro if forgery is out of scope.
300	        warnings.warn(
301	            "run_study provenance trail is UNKEYED (secret=None) — tamper-evident but not "
302	            "forgery-resistant. Pass secret=<bytes> to HMAC-key the trail for audit trails that "
303	            "cross a trust boundary.",
304	            stacklevel=2,
305	        )
306	    defn = plan.definition
307	    modes = defn.modes
308	    required = required_phases(modes)
309	    trail = trail if trail is not None else ProvenanceTrail(secret=secret, clock=clock)
310	    visited: list[Phase] = []
311	    run_id: str | None = None
312	
313	    def enter(phase: Phase, **payload: Any) -> None:
314	        visited.append(phase)
315	        trail.record("phase", f"enter {phase.name}", phase=phase.name, **payload)
316	
317	    def track(fn: Callable[[ExperimentTracker, str], None]) -> None:
318	        # observability is best-effort: a tracker failure is recorded but never aborts a run.
319	        if tracker is None or run_id is None:
320	            return
321	        try:
322	            fn(tracker, run_id)
323	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — tracker is an external, optional backend
324	            trail.record("tracking_error", f"experiment tracker call failed: {exc}")
325	
326	    def run_gate(review: GateReview) -> None:
327	        # Record the decision once (snapshot approved/reason) and block on rejection (#94).
328	        decision = gate_handler(review)
329	        approved = bool(getattr(decision, "approved", False))
330	        reason = str(getattr(decision, "reason", ""))
331	        trail.record(
332	            "gate",
333	            f"gate {review.gate!r}: {'approved' if approved else 'blocked'}",
334	            gate=review.gate,
335	            approved=approved,
336	            reason=reason,
337	            transition=f"{review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}",
338	        )
339	        if not approved:
340	            raise GateError(
341	                f"gate {review.gate!r} blocked {review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}: {reason}"
342	            )
343	
344	    trail.record(
345	        "run_start",
346	        f"study {defn.name!r} starting",
347	        study=defn.name,
348	        modes=sorted(m.value for m in modes),
349	        research_questions=list(defn.research_questions),
350	    )
351	    if tracker is not None:
352	        try:
353	            run_id = tracker.start_run(
354	                defn.name,
355	                {
356	                    "modes": ",".join(sorted(m.value for m in modes)),
357	                    "n_research_questions": len(defn.research_questions),
358	                },
359	            )
360	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
361	            # run_id stays None → every track() call silently no-ops and ALL metrics are dropped
362	            # for the whole run. Record it to the trail AND emit a structured warning so an
363	            # operator seeing no MLflow run knows why instead of guessing (pass 3, #F-028).
364	            trail.record("tracking_error", f"experiment tracker start_run failed: {exc}")
365	            _log.warning(
366	                "experiment tracker start_run failed (%s: %s); run metrics will not be logged "
367	                "to the tracker for this study",
368	                type(exc).__name__,
369	                exc,
370	            )
371	
372	    ok = False
373	    started_at = time.monotonic()
374	    trace_ctx = ExitStack()
375	    try:
376	        # Bootstrap tracing once and correlate every phase/reasoning span to this run via baggage,
377	        # so a run's spans are findable by id and join the experiment-tracker run (#122).
378	        # Done INSIDE the try so a tracing-setup failure is still covered by the finally's
379	        # tracker.end_run cleanup — an already-started tracker run must never leak in RUNNING
380	        # state (the #110 class of bug) if bootstrap/context entry raises (#156). Best-effort.
381	        bootstrap_tracing()
382	        corr_id = run_id or uuid.uuid4().hex
383	        trace_ctx.enter_context(run_trace_context(corr_id))
384	        # ---- INGEST ----
385	        with tracer.span("phase:INGEST"):
386	            enter(Phase.INGEST)
387	            try:
388	                corpus = defn.parser.parse(plan.source)
389	                source_fp = defn.parser.source_fingerprint(plan.source)
390	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — normalize adapter/IO ingestion failures
391	                raise IngestionError(f"parser failed to ingest {plan.source!r}: {exc}") from exc
392	            # The parser Protocol return type is unenforced at runtime; a misimplemented adapter
393	            # that returns None/list/dict would otherwise slip past the normalizer above and die
394	            # at `corpus.units` with an opaque AttributeError, violating the documented
395	            # "Raises IngestionError on a bad source" contract (#134).
396	            if not isinstance(corpus, Corpus):
397	                raise IngestionError(f"parser returned {type(corpus).__name__}, expected Corpus")
398	            if not isinstance(source_fp, str):
399	                raise IngestionError(
400	                    f"parser.source_fingerprint returned {type(source_fp).__name__}, expected str"
401	                )
402	            if not corpus.units:
403	                raise IngestionError("ingestion produced an empty corpus")
404	            cfp = corpus_fingerprint(corpus)
405	            source_version: str | None = None
406	            if versioner is not None:
407	                # The versioner is an optional seam; a storage failure (PermissionError,
408	                # OSError) must surface as the documented IngestionError, not a raw untyped
409	                # exception that the caller cannot distinguish from a fatal firewall breach
410	                # (pass 3, #F-008). Versioning is part of ingesting the source, so a failure to
411	                # content-address it is an ingestion failure.
412	                try:
413	                    source_version = versioner.put(str(plan.source))
414	                except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — normalize optional-seam storage failures
415	                    raise IngestionError(
416	                        f"data versioner failed to store {plan.source!r}: {exc}"
417	                    ) from exc
418	            trail.record(
419	                "ingest",
420	                "parsed source into canonical corpus",
421	                source_fingerprint=source_fp,
422	                corpus_fingerprint=cfp,
423	                n_units=len(corpus.units),
424	                n_relations=len(corpus.relations),
425	                source_version=source_version,
426	            )
427	            # SLO-relevant scale metrics to the tracker so corpus-size↔latency regressions are
428	            # analysable from the experiment store, not only the provenance trail (pass 3,
429	            # #F-029). Best-effort via track() (no-ops if no tracker/run_id).
430	            track(_count_logger("n_units", float(len(corpus.units))))
431	            track(_count_logger("n_relations", float(len(corpus.relations))))
432	            # Compute the corpus unit-id set ONCE and reuse it for both the split-id and feature-id
433	            # validations (pass 4, #G-023 — was built twice on the same corpus).
434	            corpus_unit_ids = {u.unit_id for u in corpus.units}
435	            # split ids must reference real corpus units (no silent partition drift, audit H4)
436	            if StudyMode.DISCOVERY in modes:
437	                assert plan.split is not None
438	                unknown = (
439	                    set(plan.split.discovery_ids) | set(plan.split.confirm_ids)
440	                ) - corpus_unit_ids
441	                if unknown:
442	                    raise FirewallViolation(
443	                        f"split references {len(unknown)} id(s) absent from the corpus "
444	                        f"(e.g. {sorted(unknown)[:3]}) — partition would silently drop units"
445	                    )
446	            # optional dataset features (recorded + returned; the baseline builder is blind-built
447	            # from the corpus, so features are provenance/output, not a baseline input here)
448	            feature_matrices: list[FeatureMatrix] = []
449	            for fb in defn.feature_builders:
450	                fm = fb.build(corpus)
451	                # features must describe THIS corpus's units — no fictional or duplicate ids
452	                # (the feature-side analogue of the split-drift guard above).
453	                if len(set(fm.unit_ids)) != len(fm.unit_ids):
454	                    raise FirewallViolation(
455	                        f"feature builder {list(fb.provides)} returned duplicate unit_ids"
456	                    )
457	                ghost = set(fm.unit_ids) - corpus_unit_ids
458	                if ghost:
459	                    raise FirewallViolation(
460	                        f"feature builder {list(fb.provides)} returned {len(ghost)} unit_id(s) "
461	                        f"absent from the corpus (e.g. {sorted(ghost)[:3]})"
462	                    )
463	                feature_matrices.append(fm)
464	                trail.record(
465	                    "features",
466	                    f"built feature matrix ({len(fm.feature_names)} features)",
467	                    provides=list(fb.provides),
468	                    n_units=len(fm.unit_ids),
469	                    feature_names=list(fm.feature_names),
470	                )
471	
472	        # ---- BASELINE (blind — Firewall A). Capture the ordering instant BEFORE the build. ----
473	        baseline_instant = _now()
474	        with tracer.span("phase:BASELINE"):
475	            enter(Phase.BASELINE)
476	            guard: ClaimBlindGuard[Any] = ClaimBlindGuard()
477	            with guard.sealed():
478	                baseline = plan.baseline_builder.build(corpus, claim_guard=guard)
479	            if baseline.corpus_fingerprint != cfp:
480	                raise FirewallViolation(
481	                    "baseline corpus_fingerprint does not match the ingested corpus — the baseline "
482	                    "was not built from this corpus"
483	                )
484	            trail.record(
485	                "baseline",
486	                "built blind baseline (Firewall A)",
487	                corpus_fingerprint=baseline.corpus_fingerprint,
488	                contents=sorted(baseline.contents),
489	                determinism=dict(baseline.determinism),
490	            )
491	
492	        discovery_verdicts: tuple[Verdict, ...] = ()
493	        scorecard: SourceScorecard | None = None
494	
495	        # ---- DISCOVERY → PREREGISTER → gate → CONFIRM (Firewall B) ----
496	        if StudyMode.DISCOVERY in modes:
497	            assert plan.split is not None and plan.discover is not None
498	            assert plan.confirm_held_out is not None
499	            discovery_corpus = subset_corpus(corpus, plan.split.discovery_ids)
500	            held_out = subset_corpus(corpus, plan.split.confirm_ids)
501	
502	            with tracer.span("phase:DISCOVERY"):
503	                enter(Phase.DISCOVERY)
504	                if not discovery_corpus.units:
505	                    raise FirewallViolation("discovery partition selects no corpus units")
506	                hypotheses = list(plan.discover(discovery_corpus))  # only the discovery partition
507	                if not hypotheses:
508	                    # discover() surfaced nothing — a vacuous run indistinguishable from a broken
509	                    # callable that forgot to return. Fail loud (pass 3, #F-042).
510	                    raise FirewallViolation(
511	                        "discover() returned no hypotheses — a discovery run with zero "
512	                        "hypotheses is vacuous and cannot produce a confirmatory verdict"
513	                    )
514	                # within-run identity uniqueness: a repeated hypothesis_id would be counted once
515	                # per occurrence in the verdict accounting (#138)
516	                _require_unique_ids([h.hypothesis_id for h in hypotheses], "hypothesis_id")
517	                trail.record(
518	                    "discovery",
519	                    f"data surfaced {len(hypotheses)} candidate hypotheses",
520	                    n=len(hypotheses),
521	                    ids=[h.hypothesis_id for h in hypotheses],
522	                )
523	
524	            with tracer.span("phase:PREREGISTER"):
525	                enter(Phase.PREREGISTER)
526	                if not held_out.units:
527	                    raise FirewallViolation("confirmation partition selects no corpus units")
528	                preregister_instant = _now()
529	                for h in hypotheses:
530	                    if h.origin is not HypothesisOrigin.DISCOVERY:
531	                        raise FirewallViolation(
532	                            f"hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r} is {h.origin.value!r}; the discovery "
533	                            "phase expects DISCOVERY-origin (data-surfaced) hypotheses"
534	                        )
535	                    vt = require_preregistered(
536	                        h, authority=plan.authority, not_after=preregister_instant
537	                    )
538	                    trail.record(
539	                        "preregister",
540	                        f"locked hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r}",
541	                        hypothesis_id=h.hypothesis_id,
542	                        digest=vt.digest,
543	                        locked_at=vt.instant.isoformat(),
544	                    )
545	                run_gate(
546	                    GateReview(
547	                        gate="review-locked-hypotheses",
548	                        frm=Phase.PREREGISTER,
549	                        to=Phase.CONFIRM,
550	                        summary="review locked hypotheses before confirmatory testing",
551	                        payload={
552	                            "hypothesis_ids": [h.hypothesis_id for h in hypotheses],
553	                            "research_questions": list(defn.research_questions),
554	                        },
555	                    )
556	                )
557	
558	            with tracer.span("phase:CONFIRM"):
559	                enter(Phase.CONFIRM)
560	                confirmed: list[Verdict] = []
561	                for h in hypotheses:
562	                    verdict = plan.confirm_held_out(h, held_out)  # only the held-out partition
563	                    # A confirmer that forgets to `return` yields None; accessing
564	                    # verdict.hypothesis_id would raise an opaque AttributeError instead of the
565	                    # documented typed error (pass 3, #F-021). Guard the contract explicitly.
566	                    if not isinstance(verdict, Verdict):
567	                        raise FirewallViolation(
568	                            f"confirm_held_out returned {type(verdict).__name__} for hypothesis "
569	                            f"{h.hypothesis_id!r} — expected a Verdict"
570	                        )
571	                    if verdict.hypothesis_id != h.hypothesis_id:
572	                        raise FirewallViolation(
573	                            f"verdict reports hypothesis_id {verdict.hypothesis_id!r} for "
574	                            f"hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r} — hypothesis↔verdict misattribution"
575	                        )
576	                    confirmed.append(verdict)
577	                    trail.record(
578	                        "verdict",
579	                        f"confirmed {h.hypothesis_id!r}: {verdict.label.value}",
580	                        hypothesis_id=h.hypothesis_id,
581	                        label=verdict.label.value,
582	                        rule=verdict.decision_rule,
583	                    )
584	                discovery_verdicts = tuple(confirmed)
585	                for lbl in ("supported", "contradicted", "indeterminate"):
586	                    n = sum(1 for v in discovery_verdicts if v.label.value == lbl)
587	                    track(_count_logger(f"discovery_{lbl}", float(n)))
588	
589	        # ---- ADJUDICATE → SCORE (Firewall A; the baseline built above is reused, blind) ----
590	        if StudyMode.ADJUDICATE_EXTERNAL_CLAIMS in modes:
591	            assert defn.claims_source is not None
592	            assert plan.hypothesis_for is not None and plan.confirm_claim is not None
593	            claim_records = list(defn.claims_source.claims())
594	            if not claim_records:
595	                raise ValueError(
596	                    "adjudication mode but the claims source yielded no claims — nothing to adjudicate"
597	                )
598	            # within-run identity uniqueness: a repeated claim_id would inflate the scorecard (#138)
599	            _require_unique_ids([c.claim_id for c in claim_records], "claim_id")
600	            track(_count_logger("n_claims", float(len(claim_records))))  # SLO metric (#F-029)
601	            # The engine fingerprints the EXACT claims it will score (authoritative), and records
602	            # the source's self-report separately for cross-check rather than trusting it (#137).
603	            engine_claim_fp = _engine_claim_fingerprint(claim_records)
604	            source_claim_fp = defn.claims_source.claim_fingerprint()
605	            # Enforce — do not merely *record* — the fingerprint agreement (pass 3, #F-001).
606	            # Pass-2 #137 made the engine fingerprint authoritative and surfaced the match as a
607	            # boolean in the gate payload, but a non-interactive gate (auto_approve) approves
608	            # regardless, so a source whose claim_fingerprint() lies about a claim set that
609	            # claims() does not actually yield completed a run with the discrepancy silently
610	            # recorded. The source's self-report is a content commitment for pre-registration;
611	            # a disagreement means the scored claims are not the set the source attested to —
612	            # a provenance-integrity breach the engine must refuse, not defer to a human.
613	            if source_claim_fp != engine_claim_fp:
614	                raise FirewallViolation(
615	                    "claims source self-reported claim_fingerprint "
616	                    f"{source_claim_fp!r} but the engine computed {engine_claim_fp!r} over the "
617	                    "claims actually yielded — the scored claim set does not match the source's "
618	                    "attested fingerprint (provenance-integrity breach)"
619	                )
620	            prev_phase = Phase.CONFIRM if StudyMode.DISCOVERY in modes else Phase.BASELINE
621	            run_gate(
622	                GateReview(
623	                    gate="review-baseline-and-claims",
624	                    frm=prev_phase,
625	                    to=Phase.ADJUDICATE,
626	                    summary="review the blind baseline and the external claim set before adjudication",
627	                    payload={
628	                        "n_claims": len(claim_records),
629	                        "claim_ids": [c.claim_id for c in claim_records],
630	                        "claim_fingerprint": engine_claim_fp,  # engine-computed over scored claims
631	                        "source_reported_claim_fingerprint": source_claim_fp,  # unverified self-report
632	                        "claim_fingerprint_matches_source": engine_claim_fp == source_claim_fp,
633	                        "baseline_fingerprint": baseline.corpus_fingerprint,
634	                        "research_questions": list(defn.research_questions),
635	                    },
636	                )
637	            )
638	            with tracer.span("phase:ADJUDICATE"):
639	                enter(Phase.ADJUDICATE)
640	
641	                def _record_adjudication_step(h: Hypothesis, v: Verdict) -> None:
642	                    trail.record(
643	                        "preregister",
644	                        f"locked claim hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r}",
645	                        hypothesis_id=h.hypothesis_id,
646	                        source_claim_id=h.source_claim_id,
647	                        locked_at=h.locked_at,
648	                    )
649	                    trail.record(
650	                        "verdict",
651	                        f"adjudicated {v.hypothesis_id!r}: {v.label.value}",
652	                        hypothesis_id=v.hypothesis_id,
653	                        label=v.label.value,
654	                        rule=v.decision_rule,
655	                    )
656	
657	                # not_after = baseline_instant: a claim-derived hypothesis must have been locked
658	                # before the (shared) baseline existed. The SAME materialized claim_records that
659	                # were gated/recorded are scored (#95).
660	                scorecard = adjudicate_with_baseline(
661	                    baseline,
662	                    claim_records,
663	                    hypothesis_for=plan.hypothesis_for,
664	                    confirm=plan.confirm_claim,
665	                    authority=plan.authority,
666	                    not_after=baseline_instant,
667	                    source_name=defn.name,
668	                    on_step=_record_adjudication_step,
669	                )
670	            with tracer.span("phase:SCORE"):
671	                enter(Phase.SCORE)
672	                trail.record(
673	                    "score",
674	                    f"source scorecard for {defn.name!r}",
675	                    n_supported=scorecard.n_supported,
676	                    n_contradicted=scorecard.n_contradicted,
677	                    n_indeterminate=scorecard.n_indeterminate,
678	                    alignment_rate=scorecard.alignment_rate,
679	                )
680	                sc = scorecard
681	                track(lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "n_supported", float(sc.n_supported)))
682	                track(lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "n_contradicted", float(sc.n_contradicted)))
683	                track(
684	                    lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "n_indeterminate", float(sc.n_indeterminate))
685	                )
686	                ar = sc.alignment_rate
687	                if ar is not None:
688	                    track(lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "alignment_rate", float(ar)))
689	
690	        # ---- REPORT ----
691	        with tracer.span("phase:REPORT"):
692	            enter(Phase.REPORT)
693	            trail.record(
694	                "report",
695	                "assembled reproducibility package",
696	                n_discovery_verdicts=len(discovery_verdicts),
697	                has_scorecard=scorecard is not None,
698	            )
699	
700	        if tuple(visited) != required:
701	            raise FirewallViolation(
702	                f"visited phase sequence {[p.name for p in visited]} != required "
703	                f"{[p.name for p in required]}"
704	            )
705	        if verify_trail:
706	            # Re-verify the whole chain before handing back the result. This re-hashes every
707	            # entry — O(N) — which is redundant for an in-memory trail this call built and never
708	            # mutated (each entry was hashed correctly when appended). It is a cheap safety net in
709	            # the common case but a real cost for very large trails, so a perf-sensitive caller
710	            # that trusts the in-process trail may pass verify_trail=False (pass 3, #F-036). The
711	            # important re-verification — of a trail deserialized from an external store — is the
712	            # caller's verify_records() on from_records(), which this flag does not affect.
713	            trail.verify()  # the provenance chain must be intact before we hand back the result
714	        # Terminal success entry (pass 3, #F-006): a phase 'report' record is not a run-level
715	        # terminus, so a trail truncated mid-run is otherwise indistinguishable from one that
716	        # completed. Record run_end ONLY after verify() passes, so its presence as the last
717	        # entry is an affirmative "this run finished with an intact chain" marker. The payload is
718	        # deterministic (no wall-clock) so the provenance-hash determinism guarantee holds — the
719	        # non-deterministic run duration goes to the experiment tracker as an SLO metric instead.
720	        trail.record(
721	            "run_end",
722	            f"study {defn.name!r} completed successfully",
723	            study=defn.name,
724	            experiment_run_id=run_id,
725	            n_provenance_entries=len(trail.entries) + 1,  # +1 counts this terminal entry itself
726	        )
727	        track(_count_logger("run_duration_s", time.monotonic() - started_at))  # SLO metric (#F-029)
728	        # Correlate the hash-chained trail to the experiment run (#G-009): persist it as a JSON
729	        # artifact on the tracker so an auditor opening the run can retrieve the provenance that
730	        # produced its metrics. Best-effort, like the other tracker calls — never aborts the run.
731	        if tracker is not None and run_id is not None:
732	
733	            def _log_trail(t: ExperimentTracker, rid: str) -> None:
734	                import json
735	                import os
736	                import tempfile
737	
738	                from assay_engine.provenance import entries_to_records
739	
740	                with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(
741	                    "w", suffix="_provenance.json", delete=False, encoding="utf-8"
742	                ) as fh:
743	                    json.dump(list(entries_to_records(list(trail.entries))), fh, indent=2)
744	                    path = fh.name
745	                # Clean up the scratch file after the tracker has copied it into its store, so a
746	                # tracked run does not leak a temp file (with provenance contents) on every call
747	                # (#H-013). The tracker reads/copies synchronously in log_artifact.
748	                try:
749	                    t.log_artifact(rid, path)
750	                finally:
751	                    try:
752	                        os.unlink(path)
753	                    except OSError:
754	                        pass
755	
756	            track(_log_trail)
757	        ok = True
758	    except Exception as exc:
759	        # Record a terminal failure entry BEFORE re-raising so the abort reason is recoverable
760	        # from the (caller-owned) trail, not lost (#109).
761	        last_phase = visited[-1].name if visited else "start"
762	        try:
763	            trail.record(
764	                "run_failed",
765	                f"{type(exc).__name__} during {last_phase}",
766	                error_type=type(exc).__name__,
767	                message=str(exc)[:2000],
768	                last_phase=last_phase,
769	            )
770	        except Exception as inner:  # noqa: BLE001 — never mask the original failure
771	            # The run_failed entry could not be recorded (payload unserializable, clock raised,
772	            # trail lock contended). Don't silently swallow — emit a structured warning so the
773	            # failure reason is at least in the logs, even though it is absent from the trail
774	            # (pass 3, #F-030). The original exception is still re-raised below.
775	            _log.warning(
776	                "could not record run_failed provenance entry (%s: %s)",
777	                type(inner).__name__,
778	                inner,
779	            )
780	        raise
781	    finally:
782	        trace_ctx.close()
783	        if tracker is not None and run_id is not None:
784	            try:
785	                # a failed run must be distinguishable from a successful one in the store (#110)
786	                tracker.end_run(run_id, status="FINISHED" if ok else "FAILED")
787	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001,S110 — best-effort cleanup
788	                pass
789	
790	    return StudyResult(
791	        study=defn.name,
792	        corpus_fingerprint=cfp,
793	        baseline=baseline,
794	        discovery_verdicts=discovery_verdicts,
795	        scorecard=scorecard,
796	        phases=tuple(visited),
797	        provenance=trail.entries,
798	        source_version=source_version,
799	        feature_matrices=tuple(feature_matrices),
800	        experiment_run_id=run_id,
801	    )

content.content:
1	"""Append-only provenance trail (GOVERNANCE §3, W3C PROV-DM style).
2	
3	Every action a study takes is recorded, in order, before the next executes: ingestion, the
4	blind baseline build, each discovered/locked hypothesis, every gate decision, each verdict,
5	the source scorecard, the final report. The trail is the spine of reproducibility and of the
6	independence claim — "no step can be retroactively removed or reordered" (GOVERNANCE §3).
7	
8	The trail enforces that structurally, not by promise:
9	
10	- **Append-only.** Entries are added through :meth:`ProvenanceTrail.record` only; there is no
11	  remove/edit/reorder API and the exposed view is an immutable tuple of frozen entries.
12	- **Chained.** Each entry carries ``prev_hash`` and an ``entry_hash`` over
13	  ``(prev_hash, seq, kind, summary, payload, timestamp)`` using the engine's one type-faithful
14	  serializer (:mod:`assay_engine._canonical`), rooted at a fixed genesis.
15	
16	Integrity — honest scope, two tiers:
17	
18	- **Unkeyed (default).** The chain is plain SHA-256. It is *tamper-evident against naive
19	  tampering and accidental corruption*: editing one entry without recomputing downstream
20	  hashes, reordering, or deleting an entry is caught by :func:`verify_records`. It is **not**
21	  forgery-proof: a motivated party who controls the serialized bytes can edit a payload and
22	  recompute the whole genesis-rooted chain, and the result verifies. Unkeyed integrity buys
23	  "is this a valid chain?", not "is this *the* chain the engine produced."
24	- **Keyed (``secret=...``).** Pass an on-box secret (as pre-registration does, ADR-0009) and
25	  the chain is HMAC-SHA256. The head cannot be recomputed without the secret, so a downstream
26	  store cannot silently rewrite history and re-seal it; :func:`verify_records` must be given
27	  the same secret. This is local tamper-*resistance*; non-repudiable third-party attestation of
28	  the trail's time remains the same pluggable concern as pre-registration, out of scope here.
29	"""
30	
31	from __future__ import annotations
32	
33	import datetime as _dt
34	import hashlib
35	import hmac
36	import threading
37	from dataclasses import dataclass
38	from typing import Any, Callable, Mapping, cast
39	
40	from assay_engine._canonical import canonical_json
41	from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping, unfreeze
42	
43	_UTC = _dt.timezone.utc
44	_GENESIS = "assay-provenance:v1:genesis"
45	
46	Clock = Callable[[], _dt.datetime]
47	
48	
49	class ProvenanceError(RuntimeError):
50	    """The provenance trail is inconsistent (a broken chain) or an entry cannot be recorded."""
51	
52	
53	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
54	class ProvenanceEntry:
55	    """One immutable, chained record of a single action."""
56	
57	    seq: int
58	    kind: str
59	    summary: str
60	    payload: Mapping[str, Any]
61	    timestamp: str  # ISO-8601 UTC
62	    prev_hash: str
63	    entry_hash: str
64	
65	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
66	        object.__setattr__(self, "payload", freeze_mapping(self.payload))
67	
68	
69	def _digest(
70	    seq: int,
71	    kind: str,
72	    summary: str,
73	    payload: Mapping[str, Any],
74	    timestamp: str,
75	    prev_hash: str,
76	    secret: bytes | None,
77	) -> str:
78	    try:
79	        msg = canonical_json(
80	            ["assay-prov:v1", prev_hash, seq, kind, summary, dict(payload), timestamp]
81	        ).encode("utf-8")
82	    except (ValueError, TypeError, RecursionError) as exc:
83	        # RecursionError (deeply nested payload) is a RuntimeError, not Value/TypeError — catch it
84	        # too so the entrypoints surface a typed ProvenanceError, never a raw exception (#89).
85	        raise ProvenanceError(
86	            f"provenance payload for {kind!r} cannot be serialized reproducibly ({exc}); "
87	            "record only JSON-native / hashable values of bounded depth"
88	        ) from None
89	    if secret is None:
90	        return hashlib.sha256(msg).hexdigest()
91	    return hmac.new(secret, msg, "sha256").hexdigest()
92	
93	
94	def _utc_now() -> _dt.datetime:
95	    return _dt.datetime.now(tz=_UTC)
96	
97	
98	class ProvenanceTrail:
99	    """An append-only, chained audit trail for one study run.
100	
101	    ``secret`` (>= 16 bytes) keys the chain with HMAC so a serialized trail cannot be forged
102	    without it (see module docstring). ``clock`` injects the timestamp source (deterministic
103	    tests); it must return a timezone-aware ``datetime``.
104	    """
105	
106	    def __init__(self, *, secret: bytes | None = None, clock: Clock | None = None) -> None:
107	        if secret is not None and (not isinstance(secret, (bytes, bytearray)) or len(secret) < 16):
108	            raise ValueError("provenance secret must be >= 16 bytes of key material")
109	        self._entries: list[ProvenanceEntry] = []
110	        self._secret: bytes | None = bytes(secret) if secret is not None else None
111	        self._clock: Clock = clock or _utc_now
112	        # serialize the read-compute-append so concurrent record() calls cannot interleave and
113	        # corrupt the seq/prev_hash chain (#114)
114	        self._lock = threading.Lock()
115	
116	    def record(self, kind: str, summary: str, **payload: Any) -> ProvenanceEntry:
117	        """Append one entry. Returns it. The only way to add to the trail. Thread-safe."""
118	        if not kind:
119	            raise ProvenanceError("provenance entry kind must be non-empty")
120	        with self._lock:
121	            return self._record_locked(kind, summary, payload)
122	
123	    def _record_locked(
124	        self, kind: str, summary: str, payload: Mapping[str, Any]
125	    ) -> ProvenanceEntry:
126	        seq = len(self._entries)
127	        prev_hash = self._entries[-1].entry_hash if self._entries else _GENESIS
128	        try:
129	            instant = self._clock()
130	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — the clock is an injected dependency
131	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance clock raised: {exc}") from None
132	        if not isinstance(instant, _dt.datetime):
133	            raise ProvenanceError("provenance clock must return a datetime")
134	        try:
135	            # utcoffset()/astimezone()/isoformat() can raise on a hostile datetime subclass or an
136	            # out-of-range value — keep the typed-error contract (#96).
137	            if instant.tzinfo is None or instant.utcoffset() is None:
138	                raise ProvenanceError("provenance clock must return a timezone-aware datetime")
139	            timestamp = instant.astimezone(_UTC).isoformat()
140	        except ProvenanceError:
141	            raise
142	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
143	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance timestamp could not be normalized: {exc}") from None
144	        entry_hash = _digest(seq, kind, summary, payload, timestamp, prev_hash, self._secret)
145	        try:
146	            entry = ProvenanceEntry(
147	                seq=seq,
148	                kind=kind,
149	                summary=summary,
150	                payload=payload,
151	                timestamp=timestamp,
152	                prev_hash=prev_hash,
153	                entry_hash=entry_hash,
154	            )
155	        except (ValueError, TypeError, RecursionError) as exc:
156	            raise ProvenanceError(
157	                f"provenance entry for {kind!r} could not be frozen ({exc})"
158	            ) from None
159	        self._entries.append(entry)
160	        return entry
161	
162	    def as_recorder(self) -> Callable[[Any], None]:
163	        """A :data:`gates.ProvenanceRecorder` bound to this trail (records GateDecisions).
164	
165	        Reads each attribute exactly once and defensively, so a booby-trapped decision object
166	        (a property that raises) surfaces a typed ``ProvenanceError`` rather than a raw error.
167	        """
168	
169	        def _record(decision: Any) -> None:
170	            try:
171	                gate = getattr(decision, "gate", None)
172	                approved = bool(getattr(decision, "approved", False))
173	                reason = getattr(decision, "reason", "")
174	                evidence = dict(getattr(decision, "evidence", {}) or {})
175	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — the decision is on the trust boundary
176	                raise ProvenanceError(
177	                    f"could not read gate decision for provenance: {exc}"
178	                ) from None
179	            self.record(
180	                "gate",
181	                f"gate {gate!r}: {'approved' if approved else 'blocked'}",
182	                gate=gate,
183	                approved=approved,
184	                reason=reason,
185	                evidence=evidence,
186	            )
187	
188	        return _record
189	
190	    @property
191	    def entries(self) -> tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]:
192	        return tuple(self._entries)
193	
194	    @property
195	    def head(self) -> str:
196	        """The current chain head (last entry_hash, or the genesis if empty)."""
197	        return self._entries[-1].entry_hash if self._entries else _GENESIS
198	
199	    def __len__(self) -> int:
200	        return len(self._entries)
201	
202	    def verify(self) -> None:
203	        """Recompute the chain; raise :class:`ProvenanceError` on any inconsistency."""
204	        verify_records(self._entries, secret=self._secret)
205	
206	    def to_records(self) -> tuple[dict[str, Any], ...]:
207	        """Serialize to plain dicts (for a persistent store); re-checkable via :func:`verify_records`."""
208	        return entries_to_records(self._entries)
209	
210	
211	def entries_to_records(
212	    entries: "list[ProvenanceEntry] | tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]",
213	) -> tuple[dict[str, Any], ...]:
214	    """Serialize loose entries (e.g. ``StudyResult.provenance``) to plain dicts (#F-045).
215	
216	    The same shape :meth:`ProvenanceTrail.to_records` produces, so a caller holding only the
217	    returned entries — not the trail object — can still persist a re-checkable trail.
218	
219	    Keying is NOT embedded (pass 5, #H-017): a keyed (HMAC) trail and an unkeyed (SHA-256) trail
220	    serialize identically — the records carry no key material and no trusted algorithm marker (one
221	    would be spoofable since it is not itself part of the per-entry digest). The verifier must
222	    therefore know out-of-band whether the trail was keyed and supply the same ``secret`` to
223	    :func:`from_records` / :func:`verify_records`; that is deliberate key management, not a gap —
224	    ``verify_records`` fails closed under the wrong/absent secret.
225	    """
226	    return tuple(
227	        {
228	            "seq": e.seq,
229	            "kind": e.kind,
230	            "summary": e.summary,
231	            # deep-thaw nested FrozenDict/tuple values so the record is plain + JSON-serializable
232	            # (#F-045): dict(e.payload) only shallow-converts and leaves nested FrozenDicts (e.g.
233	            # the baseline 'determinism' map) which json.dumps cannot encode.
234	            "payload": cast("dict[str, Any]", unfreeze(e.payload)),
235	            "timestamp": e.timestamp,
236	            "prev_hash": e.prev_hash,
237	            "entry_hash": e.entry_hash,
238	        }
239	        for e in entries
240	    )
241	
242	
243	def verify_records(
244	    entries: "list[ProvenanceEntry] | tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]",
245	    *,
246	    secret: bytes | None = None,
247	) -> None:
248	    """Verify a sequence of entries forms an intact chain (order, linkage, content).
249	
250	    Pass the same ``secret`` the trail was keyed with; an unkeyed trail verifies with
251	    ``secret=None``. A keyed trail will not verify under the wrong/absent secret.
252	    """
253	    prev = _GENESIS
254	    for i, e in enumerate(entries):
255	        if e.seq != i:
256	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance entry {i} has seq {e.seq} (reordered or removed)")
257	        if e.prev_hash != prev:
258	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance entry {i} prev_hash breaks the chain")
259	        recomputed = _digest(e.seq, e.kind, e.summary, e.payload, e.timestamp, e.prev_hash, secret)
260	        if not hmac.compare_digest(recomputed, e.entry_hash):
261	            raise ProvenanceError(
262	                f"provenance entry {i} content was altered, or the wrong key was used (hash mismatch)"
263	            )
264	        prev = e.entry_hash
265	
266	
267	def from_records(
268	    records: "list[Mapping[str, Any]] | tuple[Mapping[str, Any], ...]",
269	    *,
270	    secret: bytes | None = None,
271	) -> tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]:
272	    """Rebuild entries from :meth:`ProvenanceTrail.to_records` output and verify the chain."""
273	    _required = ("seq", "kind", "summary", "payload", "timestamp", "prev_hash", "entry_hash")
274	    rebuilt: list[ProvenanceEntry] = []
275	    for i, r in enumerate(records):
276	        missing = [k for k in _required if k not in r]
277	        if missing:
278	            # A malformed/incompatible trail record must surface as the module's typed error, not a
279	            # raw KeyError, so a caller catching ProvenanceError to handle a corrupt trail file
280	            # covers this case too (pass 4, #G-012).
281	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance record {i} is missing field(s) {missing}")
282	        rebuilt.append(
283	            ProvenanceEntry(
284	                seq=r["seq"],
285	                kind=r["kind"],
286	                summary=r["summary"],
287	                payload=r["payload"],
288	                timestamp=r["timestamp"],
289	                prev_hash=r["prev_hash"],
290	                entry_hash=r["entry_hash"],
291	            )
292	        )
293	    entries = tuple(rebuilt)
294	    verify_records(entries, secret=secret)
295	    return entries
296

content.content:
1	"""Durable run-state checkpointer — local, data-sovereign (ADR-0003).
2	
3	The orchestration graph persists its full state (current phase, gate decisions, locked
4	hypotheses, verdicts) through a checkpointer so a run can interrupt at a human-approval gate
5	and resume later. That state holds the entire analysis, so the store is **on-box only**
6	(ADR-0003); a SaaS checkpointer is disqualified.
7	
8	This module is the LangGraph checkpointer factory, ported faithfully from the prior
9	platform's hardened implementation and generalized (dataset-agnostic ``ASSAY_*`` env, no
10	config-file coupling — see ADR-0006). The hardened behaviors are deliberately preserved
11	because each prevents a real production failure:
12	
13	- a **self-healing ConnectionPool** (not a raw connection): when Postgres goes away — node
14	  sleep, restart, idle timeout, a network blip — a cached raw connection is permanently
15	  broken and every later checkpoint op raises, i.e. durability is lost exactly during the
16	  interrupt/resume the checkpointer exists to support. The pool runs a liveness check on
17	  checkout and replaces dead connections;
18	- a **session advisory lock** serializing first-time schema setup across *processes*, run on
19	  the *same* connection as the DDL (a pool would otherwise check out a different connection,
20	  leaving the lock ineffective);
21	- a **once-per-process-per-conn guard** (``setup()`` is idempotent but re-locking + replaying
22	  the migration query on every call is wasteful), with an in-process lock making the
23	  check-and-set atomic so concurrent threads don't both run setup;
24	- **credential redaction**: a backend exception (psycopg/psycopg_pool) can embed the full
25	  ``user:password`` URI/DSN in its own message, so the conn string is scrubbed precisely and
26	  the exception text via URI-userinfo + DSN-password backstops; the redacted message is then
27	  raised *outside* the exception handler, so the credential-bearing original is retained as
28	  neither ``__cause__`` nor ``__context__`` (a logger walking ``__context__`` cannot
29	  re-expose it);
30	- a single shared **atexit** pool-cleanup handler (one per process, not one per call) so the
31	  pool's worker thread is joined before interpreter finalization.
32	
33	LangGraph/psycopg are optional (the ``persistence`` extra) and imported lazily; the
34	credential-redaction and connection-resolution logic is pure and unit-tested offline.
35	"""
36	
37	from __future__ import annotations
38	
39	import atexit
40	import os
41	import re
42	import threading
43	from typing import Any, cast
44	from urllib.parse import urlsplit
45	
46	from assay_engine._local import require_local_uri
47	
48	_DEFAULT_PG = "postgresql://localhost:5432/assay"
49	
50	# Arbitrary fixed key for the Postgres session advisory lock that serializes checkpoint
51	# schema migrations across concurrent initializers. The value is irrelevant beyond being a
52	# stable per-logical-schema constant; "ASSY" as an int.
53	_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY = 0x4153_5359
54	
55	# Upper bound (milliseconds) on how long the cross-process advisory lock may be waited for
56	# during schema bootstrap. A stuck/crashed peer that still holds a live PG session on the lock
57	# must NOT be able to hang bootstrap forever (#129); lock_timeout aborts the wait. 0 disables
58	# the bound (block indefinitely, the pre-#129 behavior). Read fresh on each bootstrap (#F-033) so
59	# an env var set after import still takes effect; the module constant is the default + the value
60	# tests monkeypatch.
61	_MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS = max(0, int(os.environ.get("ASSAY_MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS", "30000")))
62	
63	
64	def _migration_lock_timeout_ms() -> int:
65	    """The advisory-lock wait bound, re-read from the env each call (#F-033).
66	
67	    Falls back to the import-time module constant when the env var is unset, so a test that
68	    monkeypatches ``_MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS`` still drives the value.
69	    """
70	    raw = os.environ.get("ASSAY_MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS")
71	    if raw is None:
72	        return _MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS
73	    return max(0, int(raw))
74	
75	
76	# Brief meta-lock guarding the process-local registries below (atexit registration, the open-pool
77	# list, the per-conn lock map, the pool cache). It is held ONLY for in-memory check-and-set — it
78	# is NEVER held across blocking cross-process I/O (pool.connection(), the advisory-lock wait, or
79	# setup() DDL). That blocking work is serialized per conn_str instead (#129), so a stuck advisory
80	# lock on one database cannot block initialization of an unrelated one.
81	_init_lock = threading.Lock()
82	
83	# Per-conn_str bootstrap locks: only same-conn_str initializers serialize; different databases
84	# bootstrap concurrently. Created lazily under _init_lock.
85	_CONN_INIT_LOCKS: dict[str, threading.Lock] = {}
86	
87	# Live pools cached by conn_str so the factory reuses one self-healing pool (and its worker
88	# thread) per database rather than opening a fresh pool on every call (#144).
89	_POOLS_BY_CONN: dict[str, Any] = {}
90	
91	# Pools opened by the factory, closed once at process exit via a single shared atexit handler
92	# (registering one handler per call would accumulate handlers across repeated factory calls).
93	_OPEN_POOLS: list[Any] = []
94	_atexit_registered = False
95	
96	# Strips ``://[REDACTED]@`` (URI userinfo) from any URI-like substring. '/' is allowed
97	# inside the userinfo so a password containing '/' is still redacted; only '@' (the userinfo
98	# terminator) and whitespace bound the match.
99	_CREDS_RE = re.compile(r"://[REDACTED]@\s]+@")
100	# Strips a libpq keyword/value DSN password token (``password=[REDACTED] / ``pgpassword=...``),
101	# handling quoted values that may contain spaces.
102	_DSN_PW_RE = re.compile(r"(?i)\b(password|pgpassword)\s*=\s*('[^']*'|\"[^\"]*\"|\S+)")
103	
104	
105	def _sanitize_conn_str(conn_str: str) -> str:
106	    """Return ``scheme://host[:port]/path`` with any ``user:password`` userinfo stripped.
107	
108	    Robust to malformed userinfo: a password containing ``/``/``#`` makes ``urlsplit``'s
109	    ``.port`` raise ``ValueError`` on access, so the whole parse (including attribute access)
110	    is guarded and falls back to the generic userinfo-strip regex / a constant placeholder.
111	    """
112	    try:
113	        u = urlsplit(conn_str)
114	        host = u.hostname or ""
115	        port = u.port
116	        scheme = u.scheme
117	        path = u.path
118	    except ValueError:
119	        return _CREDS_RE.sub("://", conn_str)
120	    if not host:
121	        return _CREDS_RE.sub("://", conn_str) if scheme else "<store>"
122	    if ":" in host:  # IPv6 literal — urlsplit drops the brackets; restore them
123	        host = f"[{host}]"
124	    netloc = f"{host}:{port}" if port else host
125	    return f"{scheme}://{netloc}{path}" if scheme else "<store>"
126	
127	
128	def redact_creds(text: str, conn_str: str = "") -> str:
129	    """Remove credentials a backend exception may embed in its message text.
130	
131	    Layers, in order of precision:
132	
133	    1. exact replacement of the known ``conn_str`` (handles any password characters, incl.
134	       ``/`` and spaces) — this is the precise layer and covers the real connection string;
135	    2. a generic URI-userinfo strip (``://[REDACTED]@`` → ``://``) as a backstop for any
136	       *other* URI in the text;
137	    3. a libpq DSN ``password=[REDACTED] strip for the keyword/value form.
138	
139	    Layers 2–3 are best-effort for *secondary* endpoints (e.g. a replica DSN) the caller did
140	    not pass as ``conn_str``.
141	
142	    Precision caveat (pass 5, #H-023): layer 1 is an EXACT-substring replacement, so it scrubs the
143	    primary connection string precisely only when that string appears verbatim in ``text``. If a
144	    backend reformats it (re-quoting, reordering DSN keywords, percent-encoding the password) the
145	    verbatim match misses and only the layers-2–3 regex backstops apply — which still strip
146	    ``://[REDACTED]@`` userinfo and ``password=[REDACTED] tokens, but are not guaranteed exhaustive for
147	    every backend message shape. It is "precise when the conn string appears verbatim", not
148	    "always precise".
149	    """
150	    if conn_str:
151	        text = text.replace(conn_str, _sanitize_conn_str(conn_str))
152	    text = _CREDS_RE.sub("://", text)
153	    return _DSN_PW_RE.sub(r"\1=***", text)
154	
155	
156	def get_postgres_connection_string() -> str:
157	    """Resolve the Postgres connection string (``ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL`` env → loopback default).
158	
159	    The env path bypasses any external config validation, so loopback is enforced here at the
160	    point of use (ADR-0003 defense-in-depth): graph state must not leave the box.
161	    """
162	    url = os.environ.get("ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL") or _DEFAULT_PG
163	    return require_local_uri(url, what="checkpointer connection")
164	
165	
166	def _safe_close(pool: Any) -> None:
167	    """Close one pool, swallowing errors so a single failing ``close()`` does not abort
168	    cleanup of the remaining pools at interpreter exit (``close()`` calls a C ``finish()``
169	    that can raise)."""
170	    try:
171	        pool.close()
172	    except Exception:
173	        pass
174	
175	
176	def _close_all_pools() -> None:
177	    # Snapshot under the meta-lock before iterating (#F-020): _OPEN_POOLS is appended under
178	    # _init_lock elsewhere, and on a free-threaded (GIL-less) build a concurrent list.append
179	    # reallocation during iteration is undefined behavior. _safe_close never takes _init_lock, so
180	    # closing outside the lock cannot deadlock.
181	    with _init_lock:
182	        pools = list(_OPEN_POOLS)
183	    for p in pools:
184	        _safe_close(p)
185	
186	
187	def _register_pool_cleanup(pool: Any) -> None:
188	    global _atexit_registered
189	    with _init_lock:
190	        _OPEN_POOLS.append(pool)
191	        if not _atexit_registered:
192	            atexit.register(_close_all_pools)
193	            _atexit_registered = True
194	
195	
196	def _conn_init_lock(conn_str: str) -> threading.Lock:
197	    """Return the per-conn_str bootstrap lock, creating it under the brief meta-lock (#129)."""
198	    with _init_lock:
199	        lock = _CONN_INIT_LOCKS.get(conn_str)
200	        if lock is None:
201	            lock = _CONN_INIT_LOCKS[conn_str] = threading.Lock()
202	        return lock
203	
204	
205	def _cached_pool(conn_str: str) -> Any:
206	    with _init_lock:
207	        return _POOLS_BY_CONN.get(conn_str)
208	
209	
210	def _acquire_migration_lock(conn: Any) -> None:
211	    """Acquire the cross-process schema-migration advisory lock with a bounded wait (#129).
212	
213	    ``lock_timeout`` aborts the ``pg_advisory_lock`` wait if a peer holds it past the deadline,
214	    so a stuck/crashed initializer cannot hang bootstrap indefinitely; the resulting error
215	    propagates as a redacted ``RuntimeError`` like any other backend failure. set_config is used
216	    (not ``SET``) so the value can be safely parameterized.
217	    """
218	    timeout_ms = _migration_lock_timeout_ms()
219	    if timeout_ms:
220	        conn.execute("SELECT set_config('lock_timeout', %s, false)", (str(timeout_ms),))
221	    conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_lock(%s)", (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY,))
222	
223	
224	def get_checkpointer(use_memory: bool = False) -> Any:
225	    """Return a configured LangGraph checkpointer.
226	
227	    ``use_memory=True`` returns an in-memory saver (tests / no-Postgres). Otherwise a
228	    Postgres-backed saver over a self-healing pool, with advisory-locked one-time schema
229	    setup. Raises ``RuntimeError`` (with credentials redacted) if the backend is unreachable
230	    or the extra is not installed.
231	    """
232	    try:
233	        if use_memory:
234	            from langgraph.checkpoint.memory import MemorySaver
235	
236	            return MemorySaver()
237	        from langgraph.checkpoint.postgres import PostgresSaver
238	        from psycopg.rows import dict_row
239	        from psycopg_pool import ConnectionPool
240	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
241	        raise RuntimeError(
242	            "Postgres checkpointer requires the 'persistence' extra "
243	            "(langgraph-checkpoint-postgres, psycopg[binary,pool]) — not installed"
244	        ) from exc
245	
246	    conn_str = get_postgres_connection_string()
247	
248	    # Fast path: reuse a live, self-healing pool for this conn_str rather than opening a new pool
249	    # (and its background worker thread) on every call (#144). The liveness check on checkout
250	    # replaces dead connections, so a cached pool stays valid across transient outages.
251	    cached = _cached_pool(conn_str)
252	    if cached is not None:
253	        return PostgresSaver(cast(Any, cached))
254	
255	    # Serialize bootstrap PER conn_str — NOT process-globally (#129). Unrelated databases
256	    # initialize concurrently, and a stuck advisory lock on one conn_str can never block init of
257	    # another. The brief meta-lock (_init_lock) is only taken inside the helpers, never held
258	    # across the blocking advisory-lock wait / DDL below.
259	    with _conn_init_lock(conn_str):
260	        cached = _cached_pool(conn_str)  # another thread may have built it while we waited
261	        if cached is not None:
262	            return PostgresSaver(cast(Any, cached))
263	
264	        failure: str | None = None
265	        pool: Any = None
266	        try:
267	            # Pool bounds are env-tunable (#F-040): the reasoning seam already runs 8 worker
268	            # threads, so a fixed max_size=8 can be saturated under concurrent reasoning+checkpoint
269	            # load on larger hardware. connect_timeout bounds an individual TCP connect so a
270	            # black-holed host fails fast instead of hanging bootstrap for the OS default.
271	            min_size = max(0, int(os.environ.get("ASSAY_POOL_MIN_SIZE", "1")))
272	            max_size = max(min_size or 1, int(os.environ.get("ASSAY_POOL_MAX_SIZE", "8")))
273	            connect_timeout = max(1, int(os.environ.get("ASSAY_POOL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT", "10")))
274	            pool = ConnectionPool(
275	                conn_str,
276	                min_size=min_size,
277	                max_size=max_size,
278	                kwargs={
279	                    "autocommit": True,
280	                    "prepare_threshold": 0,
281	                    "row_factory": dict_row,
282	                    "connect_timeout": connect_timeout,
283	                },
284	                check=ConnectionPool.check_connection,  # liveness check on checkout (self-healing)
285	                open=True,
286	            )
287	            _register_pool_cleanup(pool)
288	            # Bootstrap the schema once per process per conn_str. The advisory lock and the DDL
289	            # must run on the SAME connection (pg_advisory_lock is session-scoped); a pool-backed
290	            # setup() would lock one connection and DDL on another, leaving the lock ineffective.
291	            with pool.connection() as conn:
292	                _acquire_migration_lock(conn)  # bounded wait so a stuck peer can't hang us (#129)
293	                try:
294	                    # row_factory=dict_row makes this connection dict-rowed at runtime
295	                    # (what PostgresSaver requires); the cast asserts that runtime truth
296	                    # the type system can't infer from the pool kwargs.
297	                    PostgresSaver(cast(Any, conn)).setup()  # DDL on the locked connection
298	                finally:
299	                    # Best-effort unlock; the lock auto-releases when the connection
300	                    # returns to the pool, and raising here would mask a setup() error.
301	                    try:
302	                        conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_unlock(%s)", (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY,))
303	                    except Exception:
304	                        pass
305	                    # Reset the session lock_timeout GUC (#G-002): the autocommit connection set it
306	                    # at SESSION scope for the bootstrap, then returns to the pool and is reused for
307	                    # all later checkpoint reads/writes — which would otherwise silently inherit a
308	                    # non-default lock_timeout the operator never configured for normal ops.
309	                    try:
310	                        conn.execute("RESET lock_timeout")
311	                    except Exception:
312	                        pass
313	            with _init_lock:
314	                _POOLS_BY_CONN[conn_str] = pool  # cache for reuse on later calls (#144)
315	            return PostgresSaver(cast(Any, pool))  # dict-rowed pool (row_factory=dict_row)
316	        except Exception as exc:
317	            # Build the redacted message here, but raise it OUTSIDE the handler (below) so the
318	            # original — credential-bearing — exception is retained as neither __cause__ NOR
319	            # __context__ (a logger walking __context__ would otherwise re-expose the password).
320	            failure = (
321	                f"Postgres checkpointer connection failed (is Postgres running on "
322	                f"{_sanitize_conn_str(conn_str)}?): {redact_creds(str(exc), conn_str)}. "
323	                "For tests without Postgres, use get_checkpointer(use_memory=True)."
324	            )
325	            # The pool may have opened before bootstrap failed — close it and drop it from the
326	            # cleanup registry so a failed init does not leak the connection/worker thread (#105).
327	            if pool is not None:
328	                _safe_close(pool)
329	                with _init_lock:
330	                    if pool in _OPEN_POOLS:
331	                        _OPEN_POOLS.remove(pool)
332	        raise RuntimeError(failure)
333	
334	
335	def configured_checkpointer(use_memory: bool | None = None) -> Any:
336	    """Return the checkpointer the environment selects.
337	
338	    ``use_memory`` (or ``ASSAY_CHECKPOINT_BACKEND=memory``) chooses the in-memory saver;
339	    otherwise Postgres.
340	    """
341	    if use_memory is None:
342	        use_memory = os.environ.get("ASSAY_CHECKPOINT_BACKEND", "postgres").lower() == "memory"
343	    return get_checkpointer(use_memory=use_memory)
344

1	"""Analysis-graph assembly + run/resume — the goal-agnostic orchestration wiring.
2	
3	The concrete nodes and topology of a study's analysis graph are the study's (adapter's)
4	responsibility — they encode dataset specifics and so must not live in the engine (ADR-0002).
5	The engine provides the reusable, hardened mechanics around them:
6	
7	- compile a LangGraph ``StateGraph`` with the durable checkpointer attached (so a run can
8	  interrupt at a gate and resume later);
9	- bound non-converging gate-rejection loops with a recursion limit, so a run that never
10	  converges fails loud (``GraphRecursionError``) instead of looping unbounded;
11	- stamp ``run_id`` as the checkpointer ``thread_id`` and onto traces (cross-store
12	  correlation), inside a trace context;
13	- the gate interrupt/resume protocol with its correlation guard (see
14	  :mod:`assay_engine.orchestration.gatenode`).
15	
16	LangGraph is an optional extra (``orchestration``, ADR-0006), imported lazily.
17	"""
18	
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	
21	from typing import Any, Callable
22	
23	from assay_engine.observability.tracing import bootstrap_tracing, run_trace_context
24	
25	# Upper bound on graph super-steps per run. Bounds a gate-rejection→re-analysis loop so a
26	# non-converging run terminates loudly instead of hanging.
27	RECURSION_LIMIT = 50
28	
29	
30	def _require_langgraph() -> Any:
31	    try:
32	        import langgraph  # noqa: F401
33	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
34	        raise RuntimeError(
35	            "orchestration requires the 'orchestration' extra (langgraph) — not installed"
36	        ) from exc
37	    return langgraph
38	
39	
40	def compile_graph(
41	    state_schema: Any,
42	    *,
43	    build: Callable[[Any], None],
44	    checkpointer: Any | None = None,
45	    requires_checkpointer: bool = False,
46	) -> Any:
47	    """Compile a study's analysis graph.
48	
49	    ``build(builder)`` is a study-supplied callback that adds the study's nodes, edges, and
50	    entry point to the LangGraph ``StateGraph`` builder; the engine owns construction and
51	    attaching the durable ``checkpointer``. Returns the compiled graph.
52	
53	    Set ``requires_checkpointer=True`` for any graph containing gate nodes: ``interrupt`` is a
54	    silent no-op without a checkpointer, so a gate graph compiled without one would never park
55	    for the operator. The engine fails loud rather than ship a silently-broken governance
56	    graph (audit #G3). The engine cannot introspect the study-supplied ``build`` callback for
57	    gate nodes, so this is the caller's responsibility: **any study wiring**
58	    :func:`~assay_engine.orchestration.gatenode.make_gate_node` **must pass
59	    ``requires_checkpointer=True``** (see GOVERNANCE.md "Gate interrupt/resume protocol").
60	
61	    Provenance note (#111): a gate node records its decision into LangGraph ``gate_decisions``
62	    state, which is **not** the hash-chained, tamper-evident trail. To get GOVERNANCE §3
63	    integrity on the durable path, pass ``make_gate_node`` a ``recorder`` that writes each
64	    decision into a :class:`~assay_engine.provenance.ProvenanceTrail`; without it, the graph
65	    path's gate decisions live only as graph state.
66	    """
67	    if requires_checkpointer and checkpointer is None:
68	        raise RuntimeError(
69	            "compile_graph(requires_checkpointer=True): a gate-bearing graph needs a durable "
70	            "checkpointer — interrupt() cannot park the run for the operator without one"
71	        )
72	    _require_langgraph()
73	    from langgraph.graph import StateGraph
74	
75	    builder = StateGraph(state_schema)
76	    build(builder)
77	    if checkpointer is not None:
78	        return builder.compile(checkpointer=checkpointer)
79	    return builder.compile()
80	
81	
82	def _thread_config(run_id: str, recursion_limit: int) -> dict[str, Any]:
83	    # Resume/run are keyed ONLY by thread_id — the checkpointer loads the latest (interrupted)
84	    # state itself. We deliberately do NOT thread a checkpoint_id: passing one is the
85	    # time-travel/replay API, and Command(resume=...) WITH a checkpoint_id replays from that
86	    # historical checkpoint instead of resuming the interrupt (version-dependent; LangGraph
87	    # issue #7361) — which would re-execute already-completed gates and break governance order.
88	    return {
89	        "configurable": {"thread_id": run_id},
90	        "recursion_limit": recursion_limit,
91	    }
92	
93	
94	def run(
95	    graph: Any,
96	    state: Any,
97	    *,
98	    run_id: str,
99	    recursion_limit: int = RECURSION_LIMIT,
100	    trace: bool = True,
101	) -> Any:
102	    """Invoke a compiled graph for ``run_id`` (the checkpointer thread id), within a trace
103	    context, bounded by ``recursion_limit``. The run parks at the first gate ``interrupt``;
104	    resume it with :func:`resume`.
105	
106	    Uses ``durability="sync"``: a governance run must have its parked-at-gate checkpoint written
107	    *before* the operator is notified, so a crash in the (otherwise async) write window cannot
108	    lose the interrupt. (The LangGraph default is ``"async"``.)
109	    """
110	    if trace:
111	        bootstrap_tracing()
112	    config = _thread_config(run_id, recursion_limit)
113	    with run_trace_context(run_id):
114	        return graph.invoke(state, config=config, durability="sync")
115	
116	
117	def resume(
118	    graph: Any,
119	    *,
120	    run_id: str,
121	    gate_id: str,
122	    decision: str,
123	    rationale: str,
124	    recursion_limit: int = RECURSION_LIMIT,
125	    trace: bool = True,
126	) -> Any:
127	    """Resume a run parked at a gate with the operator's decision.
128	
129	    ``gate_id`` is the gate the operator intended to decide (from the governance channel) and
130	    is REQUIRED — the gate node's correlation guard rejects it if it does not match the parked
131	    gate, so a stale/misrouted decision cannot resume the wrong gate. Must use the SAME
132	    checkpointer the run started with (``thread_id == run_id``); resume always continues the
133	    latest interrupted state for the thread (no checkpoint_id — see :func:`_thread_config`).
134	    """
135	    _require_langgraph()
136	    from langgraph.types import Command
137	
138	    if trace:
139	        bootstrap_tracing()
140	    config = _thread_config(run_id, recursion_limit)
141	    resume_value = {"gate_id": gate_id, "decision": decision, "rationale": rationale}
142	    with run_trace_context(run_id):
143	        return graph.invoke(Command(resume=resume_value), config=config, durability="sync")
144

1	"""High-stakes (T3) stream-drain + error classification — exercised via synthetic SDK messages.
2	
3	The real `_high_stakes_complete._drain` and its 429/auth/error/empty classification is the most
4	dangerous untested code (it adjudicates high-stakes verdicts and maps subscription rate
5	windows). Validating it against the real frontier costs a metered call and only runs when
6	`ASSAY_RUN_HIGH_STAKES_TESTS=1`. Here we drive the REAL drain logic by injecting a fake
7	`claude_agent_sdk` whose `query` yields synthetic `AssistantMessage`/`RateLimitEvent`/
8	`ResultMessage` objects — covering the classification branches in CI with no metered call.
9	
10	Requires anyio (the reasoning extra is async); skips otherwise.
11	"""
12	
13	from __future__ import annotations
14	
15	import sys
16	import types
17	
18	import pytest
19	
20	pytest.importorskip("anyio")
21	
22	from assay_engine.reasoning import seam as rc  # noqa: E402
23	from assay_engine.reasoning.seam import (  # noqa: E402
24	    PermanentReasoningError,
25	    RateLimitError,
26	    ReasoningError,
27	)
28	
29	
30	class _TextBlock:
31	    def __init__(self, text):
32	        self.text = text
33	
34	
35	class _AssistantMessage:
36	    def __init__(self, content):
37	        self.content = content
38	
39	
40	class _ResultMessage:
41	    def __init__(self, is_error=False, api_error_status=None, errors=None, stop_reason=None):
42	        self.is_error = is_error
43	        self.api_error_status = api_error_status
44	        self.errors = errors
45	        self.stop_reason = stop_reason
46	
47	
48	class RateLimitEvent:  # name matters: the engine matches by type(msg).__name__ (real SDK contract)
49	    def __init__(self, status):
50	        self.rate_limit_info = types.SimpleNamespace(status=status)
51	
52	
53	class _ClaudeAgentOptions:
54	    def __init__(self, **kw):
55	        self.__dict__.update(kw)
56	
57	
58	_HANG = object()  # sentinel: query awaits "forever" to simulate a hung subprocess (#127)
59	
60	
61\[REDACTED]
62	def fake_sdk(monkeypatch):
63	    """Inject a fake claude_agent_sdk whose `query` yields a scripted message stream.
64	
65	    Exposes the captured ClaudeAgentOptions via ``scripted.captured_options`` so tests can assert
66	    the security wiring (env=scrubbed_env, sandbox flags) actually reaches the subprocess.
67	    """
68	    import anyio
69	
70	    class _Scripted(list):
71	        captured_options = None  # query records the ClaudeAgentOptions it received here
72	
73	    scripted = _Scripted()
74	
75	    async def query(prompt, options):  # noqa: ANN001
76	        scripted.captured_options = options
77	        for msg in scripted:
78	            if msg is _HANG:  # simulate a hung subprocess; inner fail_after must cancel us
79	                await anyio.sleep(3600)
80	            if isinstance(msg, Exception):  # simulate the SDK/transport raising mid-stream
81	                raise msg
82	            yield msg

1	"""Local vector store seam (ADR-0003) — loopback-enforced Qdrant factory.
2	
3	Ported and generalized from the prior platform. The client is constructed only against a
4	loopback host; the guard fires *before* importing the (optional ``persistence`` extra)
5	``qdrant_client``, so a misconfigured remote host is rejected regardless of whether the
6	package is installed.
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	import os
12	import uuid
13	from typing import Any, Protocol, Sequence
14	
15	from assay_engine._local import require_loopback_host
16	
17	VECTOR_HOST = os.environ.get("ASSAY_VECTOR_HOST", "localhost")
18	
19	
20	def _int_env(name: str, default: str) -> int:
21	    """Parse an int env var with a clear error naming the var (#H-022).
22	
23	    A bare ``int(os.environ[...])`` at import time raises an opaque ``ValueError: invalid literal
24	    for int()`` that doesn't say WHICH var was misconfigured, and it fires during import.
25	    """
26	    raw = os.environ.get(name, default)
27	    try:
28	        return int(raw)
29	    except (ValueError, TypeError) as exc:
30	        raise ValueError(f"{name} must be an integer; got {raw!r}") from exc
31	
32	
33	VECTOR_HTTP_PORT = _int_env("ASSAY_VECTOR_HTTP_PORT", "6333")
34	
35	# Stable namespace for mapping the engine's string ids onto Qdrant point UUIDs.
36	_POINT_NS = uuid.UUID("a55a9e00-0000-4000-8000-000000000001")
37	
38	
39	# Structural Protocol only — adapter/seam validation is behavior-based, not isinstance (#148).
40	class VectorStore(Protocol):
41	    def upsert(self, ids: Sequence[str], vectors: Sequence[Sequence[float]]) -> None: ...
42	    def query(self, vector: Sequence[float], k: int) -> list[tuple[str, float]]: ...
43	
44	
45	def vector_store_url() -> str:
46	    host = require_loopback_host(VECTOR_HOST, what="vector store host")
47	    return f"http://{host}:{VECTOR_HTTP_PORT}"
48	
49	
50	def get_qdrant_client() -> Any:
51	    """Construct a loopback-only Qdrant client (lazy import of the optional extra)."""
52	    host = require_loopback_host(VECTOR_HOST, what="vector store host")  # before any import
53	    try:
54	        from qdrant_client import QdrantClient
55	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
56	        raise RuntimeError(
57	            "vector store requires the 'persistence' extra (qdrant-client) — not installed"
58	        ) from exc
59	    return QdrantClient(host=host, port=VECTOR_HTTP_PORT)
60	
61	
62	def _point_id(ref: str) -> str:
63	    """Deterministic Qdrant point UUID for an engine string id (Qdrant ids must be int/UUID)."""
64	    return str(uuid.uuid5(_POINT_NS, ref))
65	
66	
67	class QdrantVectorStore:
68	    """Reference :class:`VectorStore` over a loopback Qdrant collection (cosine).
69	
70	    The engine's ids are arbitrary strings; Qdrant point ids must be int/UUID, so each id is
71	    mapped to a deterministic UUID and the original id is stored in the point payload (and
72	    returned by ``query``). This is a goal-agnostic reference implementation; a study may
73	    supply its own ``VectorStore`` (collection naming, distance, sharding are study choices).
74	    """
75	
76	    def __init__(self, collection: str, dim: int, *, client: Any | None = None) -> None:
77	        self._collection = collection
78	        self._dim = dim
79	        # Track ownership: only close a client we created ourselves; an injected client is the
80	        # caller's to manage (#117).
81	        self._owns_client = client is None
82	        self._client = client if client is not None else get_qdrant_client()
83	
84	    def close(self) -> None:
85	        """Close the underlying client if this store created it (no-op for an injected one)."""
86	        if self._owns_client:
87	            close = getattr(self._client, "close", None)
88	            if callable(close):
89	                close()
90	
91	    def __enter__(self) -> "QdrantVectorStore":
92	        return self
93	
94	    def __exit__(self, *_exc: object) -> None:
95	        self.close()
96	
97	    def ensure_collection(self) -> None:
98	        from qdrant_client.models import Distance, VectorParams
99	
100	        if not self._client.collection_exists(self._collection):
101	            self._client.create_collection(
102	                self._collection,
103	                vectors_config=VectorParams(size=self._dim, distance=Distance.COSINE),
104	            )
105	
106	    def upsert(
107	        self, ids: Sequence[str], vectors: Sequence[Sequence[float]], *, batch_size: int = 256
108	    ) -> None:
109	        from qdrant_client.models import PointStruct
110	
111	        if len(ids) != len(vectors):
112	            raise ValueError("ids and vectors must have equal length")
113	        if batch_size < 1:
114	            raise ValueError("batch_size must be >= 1")
115	        # Build each batch of PointStructs lazily, so only one batch is materialized in memory at
116	        # a time (bounding BOTH payload AND memory) rather than boxing the whole corpus up front
117	        # (#118). Qdrant guidance: don't upload point-by-point, but bound the batch.
118	        for start in range(0, len(ids), batch_size):
119	            batch = [
120	                PointStruct(id=_point_id(ids[j]), vector=list(vectors[j]), payload={"ref": ids[j]})
121	                for j in range(start, min(start + batch_size, len(ids)))
122	            ]
123	            self._client.upsert(self._collection, points=batch)
124	
125	    def query(self, vector: Sequence[float], k: int) -> list[tuple[str, float]]:
126	        if k <= 0:
127	            raise ValueError(f"k must be a positive integer; got {k}")  # #H-021
128	        res = self._client.query_points(
129	            self._collection, query=list(vector), limit=k, with_payload=True
130	        )
131	        # A returned point without our 'ref' payload (e.g. inserted by another writer) would raise
132	        # an opaque KeyError/TypeError; skip such points defensively rather than crash the query
133	        # (#H-021). The store only ever upserts points carrying 'ref', so this is belt-and-braces.
134	        out: list[tuple[str, float]] = []
135	        for p in res.points:
136	            ref = (p.payload or {}).get("ref")
137	            if ref is not None:
138	                out.append((ref, float(p.score)))
139	        return out
